creator: Western Power Distribution
name: ltds-common-information-model
notes:
The Long-Term Development Statement (LTDS) provides essential information to assist current and prospective users of National Grid Electricity Distribution’s (NGED) network in identifying and assessing opportunities for new or additional use of our distribution system. More information can be found at [National Grid – Long Term Development Statement](https://commercial.nationalgrid.co.uk/our-network/long-term-development).

The LTDS model in Common Information Model (CIM) contains data relating to each of NGED’s four licence areas, covering the Extra High Voltage (EHV) systems (132kV, 66kV, and 33kV) down to 11kV at primary substations. This dataset represents the first stage in NGED’s transition to publishing the LTDS in CIM format, supporting [Ofgem’s LTDS Reform](https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/consultation/formal-consultation-form-long-term-development-statement) to improve transparency and interoperability across GB networks. In time, it is expected to supersede the [LTDS Tabular Model](https://connecteddata.nationalgrid.co.uk/dataset/ltds-tabular-model).


__Scope of Current Release__ 

In line with Ofgem’s phased implementation of grid modelling requirements, this initial release provides only the Equipment (EQ) profile, which describes the physical network assets and their connectivity. Future phases will expand the dataset to include additional profiles such as: 

* Short Circuit (SC) data
 
* Geographical Location (GL) data 

* Steady State Hypothesis (SSH) and other situation modelling profiles 

* Topology (TP) and State Variables (SV) for solution modelling 

These enhancements will be delivered in accordance with the timelines set out in Ofgem’s [updated LTDS direction](https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/decision/long-term-development-statement-direction) and [Grid Modelling Guidelines](https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/sites/default/files/2024-01/LTDS%20Grid%20Modelling%20Annex%201%20-%20Grid%20Modelling%20Guidelines.pdf). 


__Purpose and Benefits__ 

Publishing LTDS data in CIM format ensures: 

* Machine-readable, standardised network models for improved interoperability 

* Greater visibility of network characteristics for developers, flexibility providers, and other stakeholders 

* Support for advanced planning tools and digitalisation initiatives across the energy sector 

This dataset is intended for use by parties seeking to connect to NGED’s network or develop solutions that require detailed network information. It is provided under NGED’s licence obligations and Ofgem’s [Form of Statement](https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/sites/default/files/2024-01/Form%20of%20Long%20Term%20Development%20Statement.pdf) requirements.

organization.name: national_grid_electricity_distribution
organization.title: National Grid Electricity Distribution
organization.description:
## National Grid Electricity Distribution is the regional electricity distribution division of National Grid.

Formerly known as Western Power Distribution, the UK’s largest electricity distribution network serves nearly 8 million customers in the East and West Midlands, South West and Wales, delivering essential power to millions of homes and businesses across its regions.

With a distribution area of 550,000 square kilometres, it’s 6,500 colleagues are committed to providing a safe, stable and reliable electricity supply and ensuring the highest quality of customer service.

title: LTDS Common Information Model

resources.name: LTDS CIM Stage 1.3 Supplementary Publication Notes

resources.description:
This dataset provides a reference mapping between substation numbers and substation names used within the LTDS CIM Power System Model. It is intended to support users who need to join or link the LTDS CIM model with other datasets available on the portal. For example: combining network topology with asset, geospatial, or operational datasets that reference substations by ID or name. 

The dataset contains:

* Substation Number – the unique identifier often used for substations
* Substation Type – Grid Supply Point, Bulk Supply Point, etc.
* Substation Name – the substation name in the LTDS CIM Power System Model

By offering a clean lookup between these identifiers, the dataset simplifies cross dataset integration and ensures consistency when analysing or enriching LTDS CIM network data.
resources.name: LTDS CIM Substation Numbers
